summary: First-principles investigation of Raman vibrational modes in B-CHap to support experimental measurements, identify characteristic vibrational signatures, and understand structure–spectroscopy relationships.
title: Vibrational and Raman Study in B-type Carbonated Hydroxyapatite
description:
B-type carbonated hydroxyapatite (B-CHap) is a biologically relevant material commonly found in teeth, bones, and fossilised biominerals. Variations in sample coloration may serve as indicators of radioactive contamination or environmental exposure.

In this study, Raman vibrational modes were investigated through first-principles calculations to support and interpret experimental measurements. The work focused on identifying characteristic vibrational signatures and elucidating structure–spectroscopy relationships in carbonated hydroxyapatite systems.
